title: Photographers, audio-visual and broadcasting equipment operators
description: Formulate design concepts and presentation approaches for visual productions and media, such as print, broadcasting, video, and film. Direct workers engaged in artwork or layout design. All artists and related workers not listed separately. Plan, design, and furnish the internal space of rooms or buildings. Design interior environments or create physical layouts that are practical, aesthetic, and conducive to the intended purposes. May specialize in a particular field, style, or phase of interior design. (Based on 26 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Direct activities of workers setting up photographic equipment and engage in research to develop new photographic procedures and materials.

Determine project goals, locations, and equipment needs by studying assignments and consulting with clients or advertising staff. Discuss production requirements with clients to determine desired images and picture composition, selecting and adjusting subjects, equipment, and lighting to achieve desired effects.

Transfer photographs to computers for editing, archiving, and electronic transmission. Load digital images onto computers directly from cameras or from storage devices, such as flash memory cards or USB devices. Develop visual aids and charts for use in lectures or to present evidence in court.
Mount, frame, laminate, or lacquer finished photographs and insert processed negatives and prints into envelopes for delivery to customers.
Develop and print exposed film, using chemicals, touch-up tools, and developing and printing equipment, while testing equipment prior to use to ensure that it is in good working order.

Enhance, retouch, and resize photographs and negatives, using airbrushing and other techniques, and manipulate and enhance scanned or digital images to create desired effects, using computers and specialized software.

Operate special equipment to perform tasks such as transferring film to videotape or producing photographic enlargements. Operate scanners or related computer equipment to digitize negatives, photographic prints, or other images and produce computer-readable, digital images from film, using flatbed scanners and photofinishing laboratories.
Perform general office duties, such as scheduling appointments, keeping books, and ordering supplies.

Select and assemble equipment and required background properties according to subjects, materials, and conditions. Create artificial light, using flashes and reflectors, and adjust positions and controls of cameras, printers, and related equipment to change focus, exposure, and lighting.

Take pictures of individuals, families, and small groups either in studio or on location, using traditional or digital cameras, along with a variety of equipment, such as tripods, filters, and flash attachments, while adjusting apertures, shutter speeds, and camera focus according to various factors.
Review sets of photographs to select the best work and examine developed prints for defects, such as broken lines, spots, or blurs.

Operate television or motion picture cameras to record scenes for television broadcasts, advertising, or motion pictures. Confer with directors, sound and lighting technicians, electricians, and other crew members to discuss assignments and determine filming sequences, desired effects, camera movements, and lighting requirements.
Monitor equipment operation to detect malfunctions and report equipment problems, ensuring that required repairs are made.

Design and modify equipment to employer specifications, while cleaning or maintaining photoprocessing or darkroom equipment and performing minor repairs and routine cleaning of audio and video equipment.
Control audio equipment to regulate volume and sound quality during radio and television broadcasts. Record speech, music, and other sounds on recording media, using recording equipment.
Prepare for recording sessions by performing activities such as selecting and setting up microphones, and set up, test, and adjust recording equipment for recording sessions and live performances.

Conduct training sessions on selection, use, and design of audio-visual materials and on operation of presentation equipment, while supervising the activities of assistants and other personnel during production.

Compress, digitize, duplicate, and store audio and video data while monitoring and logging transmitter readings and maintaining programming logs as required by station management and the Federal Communications Commission.
Play and record broadcast programs, using automation systems, and substitute programs in cases where signals fail.

Examine quality of film fades or dissolves for potential color corrections, using color analyzers and determine formats, approaches, content, levels, and mediums to effectively meet objectives within budgetary constraints.
Direct studio productions and manage background setting, lighting equipment, and camera positions effectively during filming.
Edit broadcast material electronically, using computers, while producing rough and finished graphics and graphic designs.
Keep logs of recordings and analyze and maintain data logs for audio-visual activities.
Instruct camera operators regarding camera setups, angles, distances, and movement for filming.
Provide supportive materials for exhibits and displays, such as press kits, advertising, publicity notices, posters, brochures, catalogues, and invitations.
Organize recording sessions and prepare areas, such as radio booths and television stations, for recording.
Analyze and maintain data logs for audio-visual activities, and develop employee work schedules.
Set up and operate electric news gathering (ENG) microwave vehicles to gather and edit raw footage on location to send to television affiliates for broadcast.
Discuss production requirements with clients while determining the number and type of microphones needed for recording.
Construct and position properties, sets, lighting equipment, and other equipment for filming.
Edit videotapes by erasing and removing portions of programs and adding video or sound as required, while monitoring incoming and outgoing pictures and sound feeds to ensure quality.
Retouch photographic negatives or original prints to correct defects, while splicing broken or separated film and mounting film on reels.
Instruct trainees in use of television production equipment, filming of events, and copying and editing graphics or sound onto videotape.
Perform narration of productions or present announcements as needed.
Stay current with new technologies in the field by reading trade magazines and literature on photography and broadcasting.

Review performer information, such as photos, resumes, voice tapes, videos, and union membership, to decide whom to audition for parts; read scripts and confer with producers to determine the types and numbers of performers required for a given production; select performers for roles or submit lists of suitable performers to producers or directors for final selection; prepare actors for auditions by providing scripts and information about roles and casting requirements; maintain talent files that include information such as performers' specialties, past performances, and availability; serve as liaisons between directors, actors, and agents; attend or view productions to maintain knowledge of available actors; negotiate contract agreements with performers, with agents, or between performers and agents or production companies; locate performers or extras for crowd and background scenes, and stand-ins or photo doubles for actors, by direct contact or through agents; arrange for or design screen tests or auditions for prospective performers.

Coordinate with other professionals, such as contractors, architects, engineers, and plumbers, to ensure job success; review film, recordings, or rehearsals to ensure conformance to production and broadcast standards; identify and approve equipment and elements required for productions, such as scenery, lights, props, costumes, choreography, and music; establish pace of programs and sequences of scenes according to time requirements and cast and set accessibility; conduct meetings with staff to discuss production progress and to ensure production objectives are attained; compile scripts, program notes, and other material related to productions; resolve personnel problems that arise during the production process; develop ideas for programs and features that a station could produce; evaluate new and existing programming to assess suitability and the need for changes, using information such as audience surveys and feedback; monitor and review programming to ensure that schedules are met, guidelines are adhered to, and performances are of adequate quality; edit films and videotapes to insert music, dialogue, and sound effects, to arrange films into sequences, and to correct errors, using editing equipment; review assembled films or edited videotapes to determine if corrections are necessary.

Plan, design, and furnish interior spaces and environments that are practical, aesthetic, and conducive to intended purposes, including selecting or designing furnishings, art work, and accessories; render design ideas in the form of paste-ups or drawings; collaborate with creative directors to develop design solutions; present final layouts to clients for approval; confer with clients to determine objectives, budget, background information, and presentation approaches; formulating environmental plans to raise productivity or sell merchandise; subcontract fabrication, installation, and arrangement of fixtures and furnishings; attend photo shoots and printing sessions to ensure that the products needed are obtained; advise clients on interior design factors; ensure designs are environmentally friendly using sustainable materials when feasible.

Supervise and coordinate the work of camera, lighting, design, and sound crew members; direct and coordinate activities of personnel engaged in broadcast news, sports, or programming; coordinate activities between departments, such as news and programming; check completed program logs for accuracy and conformance with FCC rules; confer with directors and production staff to discuss production and casting problems, budgets, policies, and news coverage; monitor broadcasts to ensure that programs conform to station or network policies; use computer-aided drafting (CAD) and related software to produce construction documents; supervise and assign duties to workers engaged in technical control and production of radio and television programs; manage staff and develop budgets for activities; train workers in the use of equipment.

Research production topics and current trends in new technology; use internet and video archives for information; study and research scripts to determine how they should be directed; attend rehearsals and production meetings; organize raw footage into a logical sequence; cut film segments to specified lengths and assemble into a continuous narrative; confer with technical directors, managers, crew members, and writers about production details; plan shot compositions, camera movements, and audio setups for programs; write and submit proposals for projects; develop marketing strategies; perform administrative duties such as preparing operational reports; conduct training; compile budgets; inspect construction work to ensure adherence to designs.

Design spaces to enhance visual effects or governing aesthetic standards; manipulate plot, score, sound, and graphics to form a continuous whole; select and combine the most effective shots; assemble film and tape for desired effects; supervise film editing activities; create custom illustrations or graphics; discuss visual effects and photography details with directors; and provide input to production planning.

Direct shows, productions, and plays; teach acting classes; assist in planning and executing fundraising activities; arrange for outside contractors; conduct film screenings; confirm and check scripts for accuracy; engage in ongoing staff training and development; perform on-air duties; initiate collaborations and negotiations with external partners.
Negotiate services with vendors and external partners; monitor program schedules and transmissions; develop new service lines; plan production values to enhance narratives.

Conduct technical aspects of productions, including checking equipment and setup; direct video transitions and effects; oversee editing and post-production processes; ensure production quality standards; monitor feedback and make adjustments; act as a liaison between departments during production.

Remove tasks not relevant to photographic, audio-visual, or broadcasting equipment operations, such as: teaching acting classes, planning financial strategies, and certain vendor negotiations unrelated to production activities.
Confer with clients and staff to gather information about exhibit space, proposed themes and content, timelines, budgets, materials, or promotion requirements.
Estimate set- or exhibit-related costs, including materials, construction, and rental of props or locations.
Develop set designs based on evaluation of scripts, budgets, research information, and available locations.
Inspect installed exhibits for conformance to specifications and satisfactory operation of special-effects components.
Select themes, lighting, colors, or props to be used, and plan for location-specific issues such as space limitations, traffic flow patterns, and safety concerns.
Submit plans for approval, and adapt plans to serve intended purposes or to conform to budget or fabrication restrictions.
Prepare preliminary renderings of proposed exhibits, including detailed construction, layout, and material specifications, and diagrams relating to aspects such as special effects or lighting.
Select and purchase lumber and hardware necessary for set construction, and coordinate the removal of sets, props, and exhibits after productions or events are complete.
Collaborate with those in charge of lighting and sound so that those production aspects can be coordinated with set designs or exhibit layouts.

Research architectural and stylistic elements appropriate to the time period to be depicted, consulting experts for information as necessary, and research health and safety code requirements to inform design.
Design and produce displays and materials that can be used to decorate windows, interior displays, or event locations, and develop ideas or plans for merchandise displays or window decorations.
Consult with advertising or sales staff to determine type of merchandise to be featured and time and place for each display, and conduct interviews for broadcasts.
Select set props, such as furniture, pictures, lamps, and rugs, and arrange properties, merchandise, backdrops, or other accessories as shown in prepared sketches.
Change or rotate window displays or interior display areas to reflect changes in inventory or promotion, and dress mannequins for displays.
Operate desktop publishing software and equipment to design, lay out, and produce camera-ready copy, and check preliminary and final proofs for errors and make necessary corrections.
Enter text into computer, select size and style of type, and place prices or descriptive signs on backdrops, fixtures, or merchandise.
Monitor, maintain, and clean equipment, and perform minor repairs.
Generate prepress proofs in digital or other format to approximate the appearance of the final printed piece, and proofread and perform quality control of text and images.
Prepare sample layouts for approval, using computer software, and convert various types of files for printing or for the Internet, using computer software.
Collaborate with graphic artists, editors, and writers to produce master copies according to design specifications, and import text and art elements using computer software.

Examine finished plates to detect flaws and verify conformity with master plates, and perform "preflight" check of required font, graphic, text, and image files to ensure completeness prior to delivery to printer.

Design or construct costumes or send them to tailors for construction, and review scripts or production information to determine a story's locale or period, as well as the number of characters and required costumes.
Distribute costumes or related equipment and keep records of item status, and clean and press costumes before and after performances and perform any minor repairs.
Examine costume fit on cast members and sketch or write notes for alterations, and check the appearance of costumes on stage or under lights to determine whether desired effects are being achieved.
Monitor, maintain, or secure inventories of costumes, wigs, or makeup, and inventory stock to determine types or conditions of available costuming.
Assertively participate in the hiring, training, scheduling, or supervision of alteration workers as part of overall costume management.
Act as a liaison between talent and directors, providing information that performers or guests need to prepare for appearances and ensuring effective communication throughout the production process.

Examine unexposed photographic plates to detect flaws or foreign particles prior to printing, analyze originals to evaluate color density, and use appropriate equipment for editing and producing high-quality images.
Operate visual effects equipment or assemble audio and video equipment only as required for specific productions.

emerging_tasks.task: Engage in research to learn technological developments and techniques or to develop new photographic procedures and materials.
Operate drones to capture aerial photographs and videos, following all regulatory guidelines.
Operate drones for aerial videography and photography during live events or for pre-recorded material.
Operate drones to capture aerial or unique angle footage for film, television, or video productions.
Coordinate the use of drone technology for aerial filming and photography.
Review contractor bids to subcontract fabrication, installation, and arrangement of carpeting, fixtures, accessories, draperies, paint and wall coverings, art work, furniture, and related items.
Correct color in photographs or digital images.
Create patterns for costumes based on designer's drawings.
